footer.cta_body: Tell us how many days you have, what bike you ride, and what you want to cross. We will answer with a possible route and the next steps.

whatsapp.label: Talk on WhatsApp
whatsapp.default_message: Hi, I want to plan a Moto On/Off expedition. Which routes are coming up, and how many spots are left?
whatsapp.tour_message: Hi, I am interested in the {tour} route.

home.eyebrow: MOTO ON/OFF · MOTORCYCLE TOURS IN ARGENTINA
home.headline: ROUTES THAT ARE NOT ON THE MAP
home.rider_alt: Motorcyclist on a loaded adventure bike, seen from behind

route_teasers.sobre-las-nubes: Salta and Jujuy in 7 days. Cross Abra del Acay at 4,895 m, the highest pass on Route 40.
route_teasers.gigantes-del-oeste: Mendoza to La Rioja in 8 days. Climb to Laguna Brava, 4,300 m, where a wrecked plane rests and flamingos walk.
route_teasers.volcanes-del-norte: Catamarca in 7 days. Antofagasta de la Sierra, Campo de Piedra Pomez, and the Taton dunes.
route_teasers.cruces-del-sur: Carretera Austral in 7 days. Paso Roballos, Lago General Carrera, and gravel from end to end.

home.metadata_title: Moto On/Off — Motorcycle expeditions in Argentina
home.metadata_description: Motorcycle crossings in Argentina: Route 40, Carretera Austral, Patagonia, and the Argentine north. Gravel, altitude, and distance for riders who want to cross more.
home.video_name: Motorcycle trips in Argentina | Moto On/Off
home.video_description: Motorcycle trips in Argentina: motorcycle tours through Route 40, the Carretera Austral, Patagonia, and the Argentine north.

home_calendar.heading: UPCOMING DEPARTURES
home_calendar.intro: Three confirmed windows to earn road this year: high northern routes, Catamarca volcanoes, and Patagonian gravel.
home_calendar.footer_note: Dates open with small groups. If a route calls you, write before the window closes.
home_calendar.empty: We are confirming the next dates. Subscribe to hear first.

home_custom.heading: CUSTOM MOTORCYCLE ROUTES BUILT TO TEST YOU
home_custom.body: We design custom motorcycle trips through Northern Argentina, Patagonia, or the Carretera Austral, including crossings into Chile. We build the route around your days, your bike, your riding level, and the terrain you want to cross: gravel, sand, altitude, borders, and remote roads.
home_custom.board_body: We return the route, pace, nights, and road alternatives. We create custom routes for your motorcycle trip through Argentina and Chile.

home_about.heading: THE TEAM BEHIND EVERY ROUTE
home_about.body: We build every motorcycle tour from the terrain up: we ride, measure, cut sections, and return until the route is ready for you.
field_notes: We measure real riding hours, not lines on a map.
We mark fuel, rest, and exit points.
We test gravel, altitude, and weather before opening spots.
We adjust again when the route changes.

dossier_items.title: Section
dossier_items.body: It stays only if it adds riding, landscape, and margin for the group.

dossier_items.title: Pace
dossier_items.body: Each day is built so you arrive whole and with something conquered.

dossier_items.title: Plan B
dossier_items.body: Fuel, rest, and exits are marked before departure.

home_about.photo_alt: Moto On/Off riders reviewing a route stop during a crossing

home_journal.heading: HOW WE TEST AND BUILD THE ROUTES
home_journal.intro: Every route we sell has been ridden several times before we name it. We test sections, cut others, and come back in another season. This is how each one was built.

tours_index.headline: ROUTES THAT CHANGE YOU
tours_index.intro: We do not publish routes we have not ridden. The days, kilometers, and altitude are measured.
tours_index.hero_image_alt: Three motorcycles crossing a high plain between salt flats, black sand, and mountains.
tours_index.empty: We are preparing the next season. Come back soon.

tour_detail.itinerary_hook: Every day adds kilometers, gravel, and altitude. You keep the count in your body.
tour_detail.route_map_heading: Cruces del Sur on the map
tour_detail.route_map_body: Bariloche, Trevelin, Chile Chico, Cochrane, Paso Roballos, Perito Moreno, and the return through Patagonia. Move the map, get close to the gravel, and see the full loop before writing us.
tour_detail.route_map_frame_title: Interactive map of the Cruces del Sur route
tour_detail.route_map_workshop_body: The final line did not come from a desk. We tested it with border crossings, ferry, gravel, and distance until the route was ready to ride.
tour_detail.route_map_workshop_cta: See how we built Cruces del Sur
tour_detail.no_departures: No confirmed dates for now. Tell us when you want to ride and we will build a window.

remote_routes.title: REMOTE ROUTES
remote_routes.body: Sections almost nobody runs. Gravel, mountains, and roads that do not show up on the map.

local_people.title: LOCAL PEOPLE
local_people.body: We eat where the locals eat. We sleep where someone opens the door.

hard_riding.title: HARD RIDING
hard_riding.body: Long days, altitude, dust, and gravel. Every day is a small conquest.

sleep_where.title: SLEEP WHERE THE ROUTE ALLOWS
sleep_where.body: Mountain refuge, village inn, tent by the road. What the route gives.

x_list.heading: THIS IS FOR YOU IF...
items: You have ridden deep gravel and want more.
A dirt road excites you more than a resort.
You ride to find out what you are made of.
The stories worth carrying are the ones that cost you something.

quote_section.hunter_thompson_quote: You will not find reasonable men on the tops of the highest mountains.

manifesto.heading: THE ROUTE DOES NOT ASK PERMISSION
manifesto.p1: We cross mountain ranges because you come back different on the other side. We do not sell rides: we build crossings earned with kilometers, dust, and nights you remember.
manifesto.p2: Every day has its price: Patagonian distance, northern gravel, Andean altitude. And every day gives back what cannot be measured in kilometers.

about.title: About
about.headline: ROUTES BEYOND THE ASPHALT
about.metadata_description: Moto On/Off designs offroad-focused motorcycle tours through Catamarca, the Carretera Austral, Patagonia, and northern Argentina. Tested routes, small groups, real riding.
about.subheadline: We design motorcycle experiences that turn you into a better rider.
about.cta_heading: TELL US WHAT YOU WANT TO CROSS
about.cta_body: Tell us what bike you ride, how many days you have, and which route calls you. We will answer with a possible window, the level of demand, and the next steps.
cta_prompts: Which route calls you and when you could leave.
What bike you ride and how much gravel experience you have.
Whether you want to join a departure or build something custom.
about.hero_heading: THE TEAM THAT OPENS THE TRACK
about.hero_body:
We are the team that designs, tests, and guides every route.
We do not open a departure until we have ridden it, measured it, and adjusted the terrain.
intro_paragraphs: We offer motorcycle tours for riders with an offroad focus, from Catamarca expeditions to motorcycle trips on the Carretera Austral.
Every motorcycle tour is different. We design ON/OFF experiences that combine road, gravel, and earned challenge.
We ride through volcanic zones, deserts, forests with lakes, snow-capped mountains, and closed yungas.
route_principles: Every kilometer of our motorcycle routes is designed, tested, and adjusted before you ride it with freedom.
We plan each motorcycle trip in detail, from hotels to stops and riding hours.
We provide support and organization so the experience stays intense and real.
We pay special attention to the human group.
about.terrain_heading: WE DO NOT RUN MASS TOURS
about.terrain_body: We do not run mass tours. On a motorcycle trip the route matters, but so do the people who share it. We build small groups so riders can help each other, ride together, and enjoy the road as a team. A great experience is not made by the landscape alone.
about.commitment_body: We invite you into an experience that goes beyond the road: gain confidence, add tools, and come back a better rider.
about.route_cards_body: Salta and Jujuy, Mendoza to La Rioja, Catamarca, or Patagonia. Four routes ridden before we named them.
about.image_alt_hero: Moto On/Off team riding a gravel route in Argentina
image_alt_carousel: Moto On/Off riders gathered during a motorcycle departure
Moto On/Off rider crossing mountain terrain
Group of adventure motorcycles on a gravel road
Riders moving along an open route between mountains
Adventure motorcycle stopped on a gravel road
about.image_alt_gallery_one: Moto On/Off riders moving along a gravel route
about.image_alt_gallery_two: Group of Moto On/Off riders during a crossing
about.image_alt_road: Loaded adventure motorcycle on a mountain road
about.image_alt_workshop: Moto On/Off riders at a route stop

commitment_testimonials.quote: Mina La Mejicana was the biggest challenge I have done on a motorcycle. The group had great energy. Everything was very good.
The group formed over those 7 days was spectacular. We have already had two barbecues since we got back.
commitment_testimonials.meta: Volcanes del Norte · 7 days on route

commitment_testimonials.quote: The group that formed over those 7 days is incredible. We are still in touch and already planning the next tour.

custom.title: Custom trips
custom.headline: WE BUILD YOUR CUSTOM TRIP
custom.metadata_description: We design custom motorcycle routes through Argentina and the Andes around your riding level, terrain, ideal duration, and the experience you want.
custom.subheadline: We design the motorcycle route around you.
custom.hero_image_alt: Adventure rider next to a motorcycle ready to hit the route
custom.permit_meta: Riding level, terrain, ideal duration, and experience.
custom.brief_heading: WE DESIGN THE ROUTE AROUND THE WAY YOU RIDE
custom.p1: Alongside our motorcycle tours, we offer the chance to create a fully custom trip. Built around your riding level, the terrain you like, the ideal duration, and the experience you want to live.
custom.p2: You tell us what you imagine; we turn it into a possible motorcycle trip, 100% built for you. We base it on your riding style, trip duration, road types, and available alternatives.
custom.brief_image_alt_1: High-altitude road in northern Argentina used as reference for custom trips
custom.brief_image_alt_2: Mountain route in Cuyo used to design a motorcycle crossing
custom.brief_image_alt_3: Patagonian gravel road for a custom expedition

brief_cards.title: RIDING LEVEL
brief_cards.body: We shape the route around your experience, riding pace, and confidence on different terrain.

brief_cards.title: TYPE OF TERRAIN
brief_cards.body: Road, gravel, mountain, sand, altitude, or mixed roads according to what you want to ride.

brief_cards.title: IDEAL DURATION
brief_cards.body: We adjust kilometers, timing, and days to the trip you imagine and the days you have.

brief_cards.title: ALTERNATIVES
brief_cards.body: We add road options, navigation, hotels, and recommended experiences by area.

custom.process_heading: FROM YOUR IDEA TO A POSSIBLE MOTORCYCLE TRIP
custom.process_body: You tell us what you imagine and we turn it into a concrete plan: timing, kilometers, navigation, hotels, and road alternatives.

process_steps.title: You tell us the idea
process_steps.body: Riding level, type of terrain, ideal duration, and the experience you want.

process_steps.title: We turn it into a route
process_steps.body: We make it a possible trip with real timing, kilometers, and days.

process_steps.title: We mark alternatives
process_steps.body: We define road types, navigation options, and variants by area.

process_steps.title: We add support
process_steps.body: If you need it, we add motorcycle transport, motorcycle guide, 4x4, and luggage transport.

includes_items: Detailed itinerary with timing and kilometers.
Suggested hotels according to the desired category.
GPX or Google Maps files for navigation.
Recommended experiences according to the area of interest.
custom.form_intro: You do not need everything closed. With your riding level, preferred terrain, and ideal duration we can start.

route_ideas.title: Level
route_ideas.body: Tell us your experience with gravel, road, altitude, sand, or mountain roads.

route_ideas.title: Terrain
route_ideas.body: We choose roads according to the terrain you want to ride during the trip.

route_ideas.title: Duration
route_ideas.body: We adjust the itinerary to the available days and ideal group pace.

custom.talk_body: Write us on WhatsApp and we start there. With a few basic facts we can turn your idea into a possible proposal.
prompt_items: The type of terrain you like.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.headline: UPCOMING DATES
calendar.metadata_description: Calendar of confirmed departures by route. Hold your spot before it sells out.
calendar.intro: Tours with limited spots. We put the human group first.
calendar.hero_image_alt: Loaded adventure motorcycles on trailers before a route departure
calendar.season_callout: The dates are not chosen at random:
calendar.season_body: They are the result of more than 10 years riding, testing routes, weather, and every destination.
calendar.board_intro_detail: Each card marks a route window: date, spots, section, and status.
calendar.board_intro_prompt: Choose the month; then we talk about the bike.
calendar.cta_heading: IF THE DATE FITS, THE ROUTE STARTS HERE
calendar.cta_body_lead: Write us with the departure you want to ride.
calendar.cta_body_follow: We will send availability, next steps, and what you need to prepare.
calendar.no_departures: There are no confirmed departures yet. Subscribe to hear first.

contact.title: Contact
contact.headline: WRITE US
contact.metadata_description: Contact Moto On/Off by WhatsApp, email, or form. We answer quickly.
contact.intro: WhatsApp is fastest. If you prefer email or the form, we read those too.
contact.whatsapp_body: We pick it up during the day.
contact.next_heading: CHOOSE WHICH ROUTE WILL TEST YOU
contact.next_body: If you are still measuring dates or terrain, look at the open departures and seasonal routes before writing.

reservations.title: Reservations
reservations.headline: YOUR ROUTE STARTS HERE
reservations.metadata_description: Hold your spot on a Moto On/Off departure. Choose route, date, and tell us your bike to confirm next steps.
reservations.intro: Tell us which route you want to ride and when you can leave. We will answer with availability, deposit, and next steps.
items: You choose a route or date window.
You tell us bike, experience, and companions.
We confirm spot, deposit, and what you need to prepare.
The route is blocked when we receive the reservation.
reservations.form_body: Use the form if you prefer to write everything together. If there is room, we continue the conversation on WhatsApp.

journal_index.title: Route Workshop
journal_index.headline: WE RIDE FIRST. THEN WE OPEN THE TRACK
journal_index.metadata_description: Every motorcycle tour is born from exploration, mistakes, and retries. Tested routes, motorcycle expeditions, and decisions made on the terrain.
journal_index.intro:
Every tour starts long before we open spots. It is born by exploring roads, getting it wrong, turning back, and finding better routes.
To launch a route, we travel in different seasons, test alternatives, and measure real timing, roads, and difficulty levels.
journal_index.proof_body: We do not improvise routes: we live them first. Our team gets muddy, tests terrain, adjusts timing, and rides with pilots of different levels so you can travel with full freedom.
journal_index.proof_photo_caption: Before opening spots, we return to the road and test the route for real.
proof_items: We show how we prepare routes and test roads before clients ride them.
We test alternatives, timing, roads, and real difficulty levels.
We run route checks with riders of different experience levels.
We keep a local contact network that tells us the real road conditions.

process_items.title: Exploration
process_items.body: Every tour is born from exploring, getting it wrong, and trying again. We design routes and variants we first had to find ourselves.

process_items.title: Adjustments
process_items.body: Routes have a direction. Running north to south is not the same as south to north: technical difficulty, landscape immersion, and experience change.

process_items.title: Real problems
process_items.body: When weather sent us back, when the map was wrong, or when a route was discarded, we returned months later.

process_items.title: Local network
process_items.body: We count on local contacts who keep us informed about road conditions and the real state of each destination.

journal_index.cta_heading: WE EXPLORE FIRST SO YOU CAN RIDE WITH FREEDOM
journal_index.cta_body:
We do not improvise routes: we live them first.
Our team gets muddy, tests terrain, adjusts timing, and rides with pilots of every level so you can travel with full freedom.
journal_index.empty: Soon. We are writing the first notes.
journal_index.how_we_test_copy:
We do not improvise routes: we live them first.
Our team gets muddy, tests terrain, adjusts timing, and rides with pilots of every level so you can travel with full freedom.
journal_index.proof_band_title: Exploring is testing. Testing is learning.
journal_index.proof_band_body: Every route we launch was lived, measured, and perfected on the terrain. We show what is truly worth riding.
journal_index.story_wall_body: We open each route to show what was tested, what was cut, and which decision ended up marking the road.

journal_post.fallback_aside_body: A route is understood when someone tells what they tested, what they adjusted, and what they chose to leave out.
journal_post.fallback_decision_title: WHAT STAYED IN AND WHAT STAYED OUT
journal_post.fallback_decision_intro: A route is designed when someone decides what does not enter. This is the part that rarely appears on a commercial route sheet.
journal_post.timeline_body: Each panel shows a field decision: leave, return, measure, and adjust until the route holds the full ride.

form.preferred_dates_label: When would you like to ride?
form.message_placeholder: Tell us a bit about you and what you are looking for.
form.pitch_placeholder: The route in your head, the days you have, what you do not want to miss.
form.success_body: We will write back in less than 48 hours. Meanwhile, read the workshop notes.
form.error_body: Try again in a while or write us directly on WhatsApp.
form.invalid_email: That email does not look valid.
